title: Competitive Wine Tasting

jokes.text: Oh, don't take that. I dropped it after the lesson on setups. The Professor is so old...
jokes.setup: Warning about a joke-writing class
jokes.punchline: Dropped after learning setups, then starts an incomplete setup himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about joke structure that demonstrates exactly what it's describing

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: I took a joke-writing class once. Dropped after learning setups.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: So a priest, a rabbi, and a minister walk into a bar...

jokes.text: I'll give you the same advice my father gave me the night I lost my virginity. 'Just pick one. They all cost the same.'
jokes.setup: Offering fatherly advice about choosing classes
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriately sexual context of the original advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - inappropriate oversharing disguised as wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, when I was your age, I learned that the key to a fulfilling education is choosing the right... electives.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You want to make sure you're getting the most out of your time, if you know what I mean. Really... stretch yourself. Try new positions.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: And always remember: it's not about the size of your course load, it's about how you... apply yourself.

jokes.text: Hmm. Foosball was full?
jokes.setup: Abed and Troy taking acting class to tap into emotional cores
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic assumption they'd prefer foosball
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff deflection - dismissing emotional growth with cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So you two are taking an acting class to tap into your emotional cores?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I figured you'd both prefer to just go play foosball or something.

jokes.text: Who Indeed: A Critical Analysis of Television's Who's the Boss?
jokes.setup: Annie excited about getting into a media class
jokes.punchline: It's an entire academic course analyzing one sitcom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Community absurdism - taking mundane pop culture way too seriously

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I got into the media class! I'm so excited!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's an entire academic course analyzing one sitcom.

jokes.text: Hmm. How do you say 'ha ha' in Italian?
jokes.setup: Pierce excited about taking wine class together
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dry response about looking up how to laugh in Italian
jokes.explanation: Jeff's subtle way of expressing annoyance at Pierce's enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Jeff, we're taking a wine class together! It's going to be amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm going to look up how to laugh in Italian so I can do it convincingly when you talk about the notes.

jokes.text: When I wasn't shielding the wealthy from justice, I was drinking their finest wines.
jokes.setup: Jeff boasting about his legal experience with wine
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to helping wealthy people avoid justice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff characterization - casual admission of moral compromise

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I've got extensive legal experience with wine. I've helped a lot of wealthy people avoid justice.

jokes.text: Pierce's has a special gym with swings and saddles. Uh, we don't... we don't discuss the special gym.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Pierce's wine cellar contents
jokes.punchline: Troy innocently reveals something sexual, Pierce shuts it down
jokes.explanation: Great character interplay - Troy's innocence revealing Pierce's perversion

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: So what's in your wine cellar anyway?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Only the finest vintages, Troy. Bottles worth thousands.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wow, do you ever just go down there and... get it up?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? No! Troy, that's not— we're talking about wine!

jokes.text: Got drunk ladies, fancy bathrooms, and a room full of free coats.
jokes.setup: Chang explaining why he's taking wine class
jokes.punchline: His motivations are theft and bathroom access, not wine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang logic - completely missing the point in character-specific way

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I'm taking wine class because I want to learn about wine and expand my palate.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Just kidding. I'm here to case the joint for valuables and I heard they have a really nice bathroom.

jokes.text: From before your chin dimple filled in.
jokes.setup: Chang brought Jeff a present
jokes.punchline: It's an old photo highlighting Jeff's aging
jokes.explanation: Chang's casual cruelty disguised as a gift

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I got you a present!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Oh, thanks Chang. What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: It's a photo from when you were younger. I thought you'd want to remember what you looked like before you got old.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not that old, Chang.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: That's what I said. But the photo disagrees with you.

jokes.text: Who is the dumpling with the nice Asian pair?
jokes.setup: Pierce noticing an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: His racist and sexist way of describing her
jokes.explanation: Standard Pierce offensive comment - in character but predictable

jokes.text: That's the first time sexism has made me hungry, but she is something.
jokes.setup: Pierce's crude comment about the woman
jokes.punchline: Chang's self-aware acknowledgment of being influenced by sexism
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly self-aware line that calls out the sexism while being funny

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'd like to see her... assets.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Hey, that's not cool. Although, I have to admit, I'm influenced by your sexism, so I kind of want to see them too.

jokes.text: Why would a woman want a Plymouth on cinder blocks when she could have a Testarossa, 500 horsepower with a six-speed stick? 'Cause my stick is ribbed for her pleasure. I think those are wrinkles.
jokes.setup: Pierce comparing himself favorably to Chang using car metaphors
jokes.punchline: Chang's brutal comeback about Pierce's age
jokes.explanation: Great character clash - Pierce's delusion met with Chang's cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm like a fine wine, Chang. I get better with age. You're more like a cheap beer—you peak early and then it's all downhill from there.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: At least I haven't turned to vinegar yet.

jokes.text: Hmm hmm, the red dragon ordered a number 37 with a side of pork-fried snap.
jokes.setup: Wu Mei just brutally rejected Jeff
jokes.punchline: Chang's racist food-ordering joke about her rejection
jokes.explanation: Lazy racist humor - in character for Chang but not particularly clever

jokes.text: When you get up close, she's very homely.
jokes.setup: Jeff just got brutally rejected by Wu Mei
jokes.punchline: His obvious lie to save face
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff defense mechanism - obvious rationalization after rejection

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I was going to break up with her anyway. I'm seeing someone else.

jokes.text: Which do you think is better? Trevor St. McGoodbody or David?
jokes.setup: Troy excited about choosing stage names
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dramatic name vs. mundane real name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy enthusiasm - completely missing the point of acting class

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna call myself... The Magnificent Thunderfoot!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Your real name is Troy Barnes.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I know! But The Magnificent Thunderfoot sounds way cooler!

jokes.text: Less of a drag. Quiet!
jokes.setup: Troy reconsidering when teacher enters
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal followed by teacher shutting him down
jokes.explanation: Quick callback showing Troy's adaptability and immediate correction

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? I've been thinking about this and I really think—

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But you didn't even—

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: A trust circle? It's just a circle. It's a circle. Sit down.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks for a trust circle
jokes.punchline: Student questions it, teacher irritably clarifies it's just a circle
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of pretentious acting class terminology

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Alright everyone, let's form a trust circle.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: A trust circle? What's that?

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: It's just a circle! A regular circle! Why does everyone always assume there's some deeper meaning?

jokes.text: That happened this morning.
jokes.setup: Teacher praises childhood playground memory
jokes.punchline: Troy reveals it was actually today
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - what seemed like deep childhood trauma is current adult experience

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: That's a beautiful childhood memory. Where was this playground?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, it was right behind my house.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: And how old were you?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Seven.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: That's wonderful. Thank you for sharing that with us.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, actually, that was today. I'm twenty-three.

jokes.text: It was very hard being popular.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to access painful high school football captain memory
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands what would be painful about popularity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - analytical mind completely missing emotional point

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm trying to access a painful high school memory about being the football captain.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The painful part isn't that I was popular and arrogant. The painful part is that I had to wake up at 5 AM for conditioning, and nobody told me I could just... not go.

jokes.text: What a loser.
jokes.setup: Abed's attempt at accessing pain
jokes.punchline: Teacher's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Brutal efficiency - teacher's complete dismissal of Abed's attempt

jokes.text: Like every time I go to say something, somebody just... My uncle put his finger in my no-no!
jokes.setup: Britta describing feeling ignored and invisible
jokes.punchline: Troy interrupts with shocking molestation claim
jokes.explanation: Sets up Troy's manipulation storyline with shocking interruption

jokes.text: Angela. Oh, well! Class dismissed!
jokes.setup: Professor asks who was the boss with great ceremony
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses class when Abed gives obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff - professor's pretentiousness deflated instantly

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: Who was the boss?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Bruce Springsteen.

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: Class dismissed.

jokes.text: He refused to drink Pinot Noir because he thought it was French for 'black penis.'
jokes.setup: Annie complaining about Pierce in wine class
jokes.punchline: His racist misinterpretation of wine name
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce ignorance - racist assumptions about foreign words

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce just completely butchered the pronunciation of 'Châteauneuf-du-Pape' and then made some offensive joke about the name that had nothing to do with French wine regions.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? I was just saying what I saw! 'New Pope' — I thought the Catholic Church was getting involved in the wine business!

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: It's CHÂTEAUNEUF-DU-PAPE, Pierce. It means 'new castle of the Pope' in French. There's nothing remotely amusing about your interpretation.

jokes.text: But it's not gonna be today.
jokes.setup: Pierce announces he and Wu Mei are getting married
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's not happening immediately
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Pierce's awkward clarification

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We're getting married.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Not immediately or anything. She's still got a husband.

jokes.text: No. No, they haven't.
jokes.setup: Pierce says stranger things have happened
jokes.punchline: Jeff's flat denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff response - cutting through Pierce's delusion

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Stranger things have happened.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, they haven't.

jokes.text: I mentioned that her breasts were larger than most Asian women, and, uh, we just got to talking.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he and Wu Mei connected
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate opening line
jokes.explanation: Standard Pierce offensive behavior - treating racism as romantic

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I went up to her and said, 'Are you a Chinese takeout menu? Because I want to fold you in half and put you in my pocket.'

jokes.text: It's the only place to get fireworks that are too dangerous for Mexico.
jokes.setup: Pierce has been to China many times
jokes.punchline: For extremely dangerous fireworks
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation - what could be too dangerous for Mexico?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: For extremely dangerous fireworks

jokes.text: Or at least until she gets heavy.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying they'll be together forever
jokes.punchline: Casually adds weight-based caveat
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce shallow materialism disguised as romance

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We'll be together forever.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, as long as you don't gain more than fifteen pounds.

jokes.text: Don't preach to me about romance, Annie. I had a three-way in a hot air balloon.
jokes.setup: Annie questioning Jeff's understanding of romance
jokes.punchline: Jeff's absurd sexual bragging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff response - turning romance discussion into sexual boasting

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I've had more romantic encounters than you've had study sessions.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I once made a woman climax just by describing my bone structure.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Another time, I seduced a girl with a single wink. She didn't even know what hit her.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm basically a sexual god among mortals.

jokes.text: Hey, don't take on his pain. Tend your own garden.
jokes.setup: Troy worried about Jeff being worked up
jokes.punchline: Britta's pseudo-therapeutic advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta - dispensing therapy speak inappropriately

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Jeff's been working really hard. I'm worried he's getting worked up.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Troy, you need to stop enabling his behavior. He's using work as a defense mechanism to avoid processing his deep-seated abandonment issues.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: He's just studying for a test.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Exactly. Classic avoidance pattern.

jokes.text: Britta's attracted to men in pain. It helps her pretend to be mentally healthy.
jokes.setup: Troy asking about Britta's interest in his fake trauma
jokes.punchline: Abed's clinical analysis of Britta's psychology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Abed observation - cutting psychological insight delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Britta's attracted to broken things because she's broken. It's a defense mechanism. She'll date you, realize you're not actually damaged, and leave you for someone with real problems. It's why she dates so much.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know. This is part of why you'll never change.

jokes.text: No, uncle Lucius! I don't want to play in the laundry room!
jokes.setup: Troy deciding to fake more trauma to attract Britta
jokes.punchline: Elaborating on the fake molestation story
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the fake trauma storyline - uncomfortable but in character

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Actually, it wasn't just one time. There were multiple incidents. He used to make me sing show tunes while he... while he did things.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Troy.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And the worst part? He had really good taste in musicals. So now whenever I hear 'Defying Gravity,' I just...

jokes.text: There's just so much darkness. And my soul is darkening, and my heart is mad at my kidneys
jokes.setup: Britta responding to Troy's fake trauma performance
jokes.punchline: Troy's nonsensical description of emotional damage
jokes.explanation: Great Troy absurdism - trying to sound deep but making no sense

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: My heart is a football, and it just got intercepted by the blitz of life.

jokes.text: Pierce is a beautiful, wise man, very gentle soul.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Wu Mei about Pierce
jokes.punchline: Her completely inaccurate description of Pierce
jokes.explanation: Ironic given everything we know about Pierce - obvious manipulation

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So what do you know about Pierce?

dialogue.speaker: Wu Mei
dialogue.line: Oh, Pierce? He's a young, athletic guy. Very into social media. Always talking about his cryptocurrency portfolio. Super trendy fashion sense. Really into TikTok dances.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: ...Are you describing Pierce Hawthorne?

dialogue.speaker: Wu Mei
dialogue.line: Yes. Why?

jokes.text: Mm, trust me, that's not what they call you.
jokes.setup: Annie says that's why they call her irony-free Annie
jokes.punchline: Jeff's implication she has a different nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff cutting remark - implying Annie has unflattering nickname

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That's why they call me Irony-Free Annie.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, the other stuff they call you is way worse.

jokes.text: It's as open as the door to Mona's bedroom. It's a shame yours isn't.
jokes.setup: Abed saying his mind is open
jokes.punchline: Obscure Who's the Boss reference as insult to professor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - using his encyclopedic knowledge as a weapon

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My mind is open.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: But not so open that I don't know you're acting like Angela from Who's the Boss?.

jokes.text: Cool. Cool cool cool.
jokes.setup: Professor sarcastically tells Abed to teach next class
jokes.punchline: Abed's signature catchphrase accepting the challenge
jokes.explanation: Iconic Abed catchphrase - perfectly timed character moment

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Cool. Cool cool cool cool cool cool cool.

jokes.text: You said if we didn't, you'd slash our tires.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying he's touched they threw an engagement party
jokes.punchline: Annie revealing they were threatened
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Pierce's sentimental moment

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm touched you guys threw me an engagement party.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, we were threatened.

jokes.text: She is funny. Like Oprah. Oprah's not a comedienne. No, you are funny, and you are like Oprah.
jokes.setup: Pierce comparing Wu Mei to Oprah
jokes.punchline: His confused logic when corrected
jokes.explanation: Weak Pierce confusion - more annoying than funny

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You're like the Oprah of this place.

dialogue.speaker: Wu Mei
dialogue.line: I'm nothing like Oprah.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, you're both... you're both very successful and influential women.

dialogue.speaker: Wu Mei
dialogue.line: That's literally all we have in common.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Exactly! And you're both... not white.

jokes.text: Don't sell yourself short. You're a baboon everywhere.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying he's like an insatiable baboon in the bedroom
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cutting expansion of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jeff takedown - perfect structure and economy

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, and like a baboon, you throw your feces at people when they get too close.

jokes.text: I always carry some spare apps in case the wait staff's racist.
jokes.setup: Chang pulling out mystery food item
jokes.punchline: His paranoid preparation for restaurant discrimination
jokes.explanation: Good Chang paranoia - specific and character-driven absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You see, I've prepared a backup meal in case they refuse to serve me.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: It's not paranoia if it's happened before. I've got lo mein in my pocket, a thermos of soup in my jacket, and emergency dumplings in my socks.

jokes.text: In China, they put the last name first. I mean, they're nuts over there.
jokes.setup: Chang explaining Chinese naming convention
jokes.punchline: His casual racism about Chinese culture
jokes.explanation: Standard Chang casual racism - in character but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: In Chinese culture, the family name comes first, then the given name. It's a sign of respect for your ancestors.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Of course, back in the day, they'd name their kids things like 'Cow Tooth' and 'Broken Stick.' So maybe the order doesn't matter as much as we think.

jokes.text: I don't want to get corn-holed on the bar tab. Pierce, you can't just throw around a term like that. Corn-hole. Corn-hole, corn-hole!
jokes.setup: Pierce using 'corn-holed' innocently about money
jokes.punchline: Annie's correction and Pierce's defiant repetition
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor - Pierce's innocent crudeness

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's not what that means.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I'm going to keep saying it anyway because I like the way it sounds.

jokes.text: Pierce! Troy was molested! Cool.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to get Pierce to be sensitive about language
jokes.punchline: Pierce's completely inappropriate 'cool' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce obliviousness - completely missing the point

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, you really need to be more sensitive about the language you use.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: What? I'm cool with it.

jokes.text: We're gonna laugh so hard about this later.
jokes.setup: Troy confessing he made up the molestation story
jokes.punchline: His assumption everyone will find it funny
jokes.explanation: Good Troy naivety - not understanding the seriousness of his lie

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I made it all up. There was no molestation. I just wanted to see if I could get away with it.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Why isn't anyone laughing?

jokes.text: Very impressive, Veronica Mars. You learned how to use Google.
jokes.setup: Jeff's elaborate toast revealing Wu Mei's corporate espionage
jokes.punchline: Wu Mei's dismissive response reducing his detective work to basic googling
jokes.explanation: Great comeback - deflating Jeff's detective pride with reality check

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wu Mei, I've uncovered your entire operation. The shell companies in the Cayman Islands, the forged credentials, the infiltration of Greendale—it's all here.

dialogue.speaker: Wu Mei
dialogue.line: You Googled me.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I... yes, but—

dialogue.speaker: Wu Mei
dialogue.line: For like twenty minutes.

jokes.text: And I was just using her to get her company in the sack.
jokes.setup: Jeff pointing out Wu Mei was using Pierce for his company
jokes.punchline: Pierce's crude pun about sexual conquest
jokes.explanation: Weak pun - typical Pierce crude wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not afraid to admit it — I conquered that woman like a European colonizer.

jokes.text: I think what you were trying to do is prove that no woman could want me.
jokes.setup: Jeff claiming he was trying to help Pierce
jokes.punchline: Pierce's surprisingly perceptive insight into Jeff's psychology
jokes.explanation: Rare Pierce insight - cutting through Jeff's rationalizations

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You weren't trying to help me, Jeff. You were trying to help yourself feel like a good person. And the saddest part? It almost worked.

jokes.text: Someone who, other than his irrational fear of automatic toilets, is normal.
jokes.setup: Britta hoping to fall for someone healthy and normal
jokes.punchline: Reveals Troy's specific absurd phobia
jokes.explanation: Great specific absurd detail that feels perfectly Troy

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm just hoping to fall for someone healthy and normal for once.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, I'm pretty normal.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Troy, you're afraid of ceiling fans.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Ceiling fans are a real threat!

jokes.text: My uncle never stuck his finger in my plop-plop. I know. I'm bummed about it, too.
jokes.setup: Troy confessing his lie about molestation
jokes.punchline: Teacher's inappropriate disappointment response
jokes.explanation: Dark callback - teacher's inappropriate response to confession

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I lied. There was no molestation. I just wanted attention.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Well, that's disappointing. I already typed up the report.

jokes.text: The pain of not having enough pain is still pain, young man.
jokes.setup: Troy's confession about wanting to be interesting
jokes.punchline: Teacher's pseudo-profound circular logic
jokes.explanation: Good parody of pretentious acting teacher philosophy

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I just want to be interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Professor Duncan
dialogue.line: You know what's interesting? The fact that you want to be interesting makes you interesting. But the moment you become interesting, you'll stop wanting to be interesting, which makes you uninteresting again. So the only way to stay interesting is to never achieve your goal of being interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: ...I hate this class.

jokes.text: Angela Bauer.
jokes.setup: Abed's elaborate academic presentation on who's the boss
jokes.punchline: Arrives at the same obvious answer as before
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - overthinking to reach obvious conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: After extensive analysis of power dynamics, hierarchical structures, and interpersonal relationships within our group, I've completed my comprehensive study on who, definitively, is the boss.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's Britta.

jokes.text: It's called Fiddla Please.
jokes.setup: Troy mentioning all-black Fiddler on the Roof production
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous title adaptation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on classic musical - absurd but memorable

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: They're doing an all-black production of Fiddler on the Roof.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: It's called Fiddler on the Roof... of Harlem.

jokes.text: They're like Chinese Mexicans.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Thai food vs Mexican food
jokes.punchline: Wu Mei's bizarre cultural comparison
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing cultural confusion - shows Wu Mei's character

tmdb_overview: Jeff is suspicious of Pierce's engagement to a pretty classmate. Troy invents a childhood trauma to impress Britta, and Abed challenges a professor.
insight: Wine episode struggles with tone—54 jokes land at only 72.9, dragged by uneven cringe execution.
